name: Southern Anillo Verde Ciclista and Getafe Loop
description: This easy 26.5-mile (42.6 km) road cycling route connects Getafe, Leganés, and Villaverde, offering varied urban and natural scenery.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Leganés benefit from the municipality's local green infrastructure and its connectivity to broader regional networks within the Community of Madrid. The terrain offers a mix of urban paths, local parks, and access to more extensive routes like the CiclaMadrid network and Vias Verdes. While many routes feature paved surfaces, some segments may be unpaved. The area provides options from easy recreational rides through green spaces to more demanding circuits with significant elevation gains towards the Sierra de Madrid. Best road cycling routes around Leganés The most popular road cycling route is Southern Anillo Verde Ciclista and Getafe Loop, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This easy route connects urban areas with green infrastructure. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Marañosa Climb – Titulcia Main Square loop from La Fortuna, a difficult 61.8 miles (99.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness levels. Local road cyclists also love the San Fernando Bridge – Manzanares River loop from La Peseta, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail leading through the Manzanares River area, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Road cycling around Leganés is defined by local green infrastructure, regional cycling networks, and access to the wider Community of Madrid's varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through parks to challenging routes with notable climbs. The routes in Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 21,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Leganés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Leganés?
A: Leganés offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 160 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy recreational rides to more demanding circuits.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Leganés?
A: The terrain around Leganés is quite varied. You'll find a mix of urban paths, local parks, and access to broader regional networks. While many routes feature well-paved surfaces, some segments may be unpaved, so it's good to be prepared. Elevation gains range from gentle, easy rides to significant climbs, especially on routes extending towards the Sierra de Madrid.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Leganés?
A: Yes, Leganés has 22 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These often pass through local green spaces like North Leganés Park or Parque Lineal Arroyo Culebro, offering pleasant, less challenging terrain.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists near Leganés?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 53 difficult routes available. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7798344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa Climb – Titulcia Main Square loop from La Fortuna&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 100 km long and features significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness levels. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa Climb – ⛰️ END La Marañosa loop from Las Margaritas Universidad&lt;/a&gt;, which covers almost 120 km with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Leganés?
A: While cycling around Leganés, you can explore various green spaces and cultural attractions. Local parks like Dunes Park, with its unique landscape, and Valdegrullas Park, known for its lush greenery and lake, are popular spots. You might also encounter the city's outdoor Sculpture Museum. For those venturing further, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/664165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Campo Lake&lt;/a&gt; is a popular natural monument accessible from Leganés, and you can also find waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Leganés?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Leganés are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977180885/southern-anillo-verde-ciclista-and-getafe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southern Anillo Verde Ciclista and Getafe Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 42.6 km circular path connecting urban areas with green infrastructure. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21384634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Fernando Bridge – Manzanares River loop from La Peseta&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 31.3 km trail.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Leganés?
A: The road cycling routes around Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Cyclists often praise the variety of routes, from easy rides through local parks to challenging circuits with significant climbs, and the excellent connectivity to broader regional networks like CiclaMadrid.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Leganés?
A: Yes, Leganés offers several family-friendly road cycling options, particularly within its numerous local parks and green spaces. Shorter, easier routes are ideal for families, allowing for enjoyable rides in a safe environment. Parks like Valdegrullas Park and La Chopera Park provide pleasant paths suitable for all ages.

Q: Is it possible to access road cycling routes in Leganés using public transport?
A: Leganés is well-connected within the Community of Madrid, making it feasible to access cycling routes via public transport. The municipality's urban nature and integration into the regional transport network mean you can often reach starting points of routes using local buses or trains, though specific bike carriage rules may apply depending on the service.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Leganés?
A: The Community of Madrid generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate, making spring and autumn ideal for road cycling in Leganés. Temperatures are mild, and the weather is typically pleasant. Summer can be very hot, especially midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter can be cooler but often still suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Given Leganés's urban setting and its connectivity to other towns, you'll find numerous opportunities for refreshments. Many routes pass through or near urban centers and local parks where cafes, restaurants, and shops are readily available for a break or to refuel during your ride.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes that start near Leganés?
A: Yes, Leganés's location provides excellent access to longer regional cycling networks. You can connect to routes like the CiclaMadrid network or venture towards the Vias Verdes (Green Routes), which are former railway lines converted into cycling paths. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa Climb – ⛰️ END La Marañosa loop from Las Margaritas Universidad&lt;/a&gt;, at nearly 120 km, offer a significant long-distance challenge.

The most urban part of the Manzanares linear park has been extended to the southeast along both banks of the river with recently paved walkways and bike lanes. The renaturation of the river has allowed the development of native flora and fauna species, making the route much more pleasant and less urban. It is important to travel carefully by bicycle, especially on days with the greatest influx of public.
It is a very large park and has many beautiful corners to appreciate. There are bike paths, a lake where you can go canoeing and other activities, and a botanical garden area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This easy racebike route usually takes around 1 hour and 59 minutes to complete, making it suitable for cyclists of any fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Getafe, Leganés, Villaverde circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, ideal for road bikes. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, so it's a good idea to check conditions before you go.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Getafe, Leganés, Villaverde circular route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of urban and suburban scenery. Highlights include the Saeta Aircraft Monument, Paseo de John Lennon, the Aviocar Monument, and a pleasant Cycle path in the Manzanares Linear Park. You'll also pass by Pradolongo Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Madrid region offers diverse routes and improving infrastructure, making it enjoyable for cycling throughout much of the year. For the most comfortable experience, consider cycling during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an "easy" road ride, making it suitable for cyclists of any fitness level, including beginners. Its accessibility and mostly paved surfaces contribute to its popularity.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route connects Getafe with Leganés and Villaverde. You can typically find parking in urban areas near the start points in these municipalities. Consider public transport options as well for easier access.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly overlaps with sections of other paths, including the Anillo Verde Ciclista, Ruta Verde 02 Anillo Verde Ciclista 2 - Mitad sur, and the Ruta Verde 3.1 Canal del Manzanares.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It passes through urban and suburban areas, and parts are near the Manzanares River, which is within the Parque Regional del Sureste, but general access for cycling is free.

faqs.question: Is the Getafe, Leganés, Villaverde circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a racebike route on paved and some potentially unpaved surfaces, it's generally not ideal for dogs to run alongside. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can be safely transported in a bike trailer or carrier, and always adhere to local leash laws in parks and urban areas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a varied urban and suburban landscape. The route connects different towns and offers glimpses of natural features near the Manzanares River, which is part of the Southeast Regional Park, and some pine forests around Cerro de los Ángeles. It's a blend of cityscapes and green spaces.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point of the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Getafe, Leganés, and Villaverde are well-connected by public transport within the Madrid region, including Cercanías train lines and metro, making it convenient to reach various points along the circular route to start your ride.
